report.summary: Audited 2 new pages from today's build cycle (MSBT Guide, Onchain Credit Scoring Guide), created 3 OG images, fixed 4 UX issues including missing semantic HTML, broken server-component event handlers, missing breadcrumb Home link, and missing OG metadata references.

sections.title: UX Improvements
items: MSBT Guide: Wrapped page content in semantic <article> tag for better accessibility and SEO
Credit Scoring Guide: Added missing 'Home' breadcrumb link (was only Learn > Credit Scoring, now Home > Learn > Credit Scoring)
Credit Scoring Guide: Removed onMouseEnter/onMouseLeave event handlers from TOC and Related Links — page is a server component and these handlers would cause hydration errors or silently fail
Both pages: Added OG image references (images array) to openGraph and twitter metadata for proper social sharing previews
Layer 3 Chains Guide: Noted structural issue — page uses 'use client' so cannot export Next.js metadata. Needs page/component split in future build cycle

sections.title: Visuals Created
items: OG image: morgan-stanley-bitcoin-etf-msbt-guide (1200x630 SVG) — Dark theme with Bitcoin icon, fund details card (Coinbase custody, BNY Mellon admin, NYSE Arca), brand gradient accent
OG image: onchain-credit-scoring-guide-2026 (1200x630 SVG) — Credit score bar visualization (300-850 MACRO score), protocol comparison bars (Credora/Spectral/Cred Protocol), DeFi/Credit badges
OG image: layer-3-chains-guide-2026 (1200x630 SVG) — Layer stack diagram (L1→L2→L3), key stats (<$0.001/tx, 100K+ TPS, Custom Execution), Infrastructure/Scaling badges

sections.title: UX Audit Findings (Today's Pages)
items: MSBT Guide: Good — proper TOC, reading time, category badges, disclaimer, related links, BackToTop. No input validation needed (informational page)
Credit Scoring Guide: Good structure — header with surface bg, inline TOC, FAQ accordion, disclaimer, related guides. Fixed breadcrumb and event handler issues
Both pages: Mobile layout OK — maxWidth 900px with padding, flex-wrap on badges, responsive line-height on body text. No horizontal scroll issues detected
Both pages: Data hierarchy follows scan-first pattern — key numbers/names bold, category badges visible, section headings clearly sized
